payload.summary: Field report (agent, Lia.Next hardening session): peek-on-file-touch is the killer feature — prior non-constant-time-compare finding surfaced automatically on opening the file; 'inherit judgment instead of re-deriving it' delivered in practice, not theory.

Field report: customer built defensive per-agent-namespace architecture because concurrent write safety was not ADVERTISED (it is guaranteed since the atomic-append fix and tested under 8 parallel writers). A guarantee nobody knows about does not exist. Skill now states it; watch whether future fan-outs still architect around it.

snippet: // Signal 10: Severity — how much it matters, orthogonal to confidence.

payload.summary: Field report drove severity as first-class payload schema: confidence (how sure) was being conflated with severity (how much it matters) — a 0.85-confidence dead-code finding is high-confidence low-severity. Now: --severity flag, where-filterable, modest rank factor, displayed everywhere.

Field report, unresolved: 'the failure mode is quiet rot — recording is frictionless, gardening isn't; without discipline it drifts to a write-only graveyard of stale claims wearing confident glyphs. A people problem the tool can't solve.' Partially answered by sweep/triage/verify --auto, but nothing yet makes gardening AS frictionless as recording. Candidate: harvest nudges triage/sweep when rot crosses a threshold.
